purpose: Defines the !toc_update slash command — incremental updater of {project_root}/.synapse/toc.md by detecting new/deleted/moved/semantically-changed files since last generation. Three-phase workflow: (1) Diff Detection — read current TOC, scan filesystem with stack-appropriate Globs, detect renames via `git diff --name-status -M HEAD~10` (R lines), detect modified files via fingerprints.json (`git diff --since={generated_at}` or `find -newer`) AND via content hash comparison against `<!-- hash:xxx -->` comments, compute new/deleted/modified/renamed/unchanged sets; (2) Parallel Scan via !p dispatch — group new+modified files by directory, dispatch agents with previous TOC entry data + previous fingerprint data for modified files (so agents produce focused diffs), agents return updated entries + new fingerprints + new content hashes; compare old vs new fingerprints to detect PURPOSE CHANGE / API CHANGE (>50% export names differ) / SIGNATURE CHANGE (kind differs or params delta ≥2) / DEPENDENCY CHANGE (imports added/removed); (3) TOC Update — apply deletions + additions (in correct directory section) + modifications (preserve position, update hash comment) + renames (carry metadata, update path, update cross-references), update Project Overview if structural changes, set Last updated date, update fingerprints.json and dep_graph.json (forward+reverse edge updates: remove deleted files from old targets' imported_by, add new files to new targets' imported_by). Handles legacy TOCs without hash comments by treating them as unchanged.
gotchas: Master does NOT read source files — agents read new/modified files; master only reads TOC, ls, CLAUDE.md, agent returns.
Only scan NEW and MODIFIED files — never re-read files with unchanged content hashes. Speed is the entire point.
Preserve unchanged entries VERBATIM — never rewrite/rephrase/improve them.
Backward compatible: TOC entries without `<!-- hash:xxx -->` comments are treated as unchanged (legacy TOCs); they gain hashes when next modified.
Renames preserve metadata: when file renamed but not modified, carry summary/tags/related/exports to new path; ALSO update Related: entries in OTHER TOC entries that referenced old path.
Renames detected by `git diff --name-status -M` — R lines indicate renames; verify old-in-TOC + new-on-disk to confirm.
Fingerprint detection has TWO independent sources: (a) git diff --since={generated_at} for files changed in git, (b) content hash mismatch against TOC. Deduplicate the union.
Semantic change flags are INFORMATIONAL — they surface architectural shifts but do not block the update. Always reported when fingerprints exist.
API CHANGE threshold: >50% of export names differ between old and new sets.
SIGNATURE CHANGE: kind changed OR params delta ≥2.
If diff is large (50+ new files), suggest !toc_generate for cleaner result.
If zero changes detected, exit immediately with 'TOC is up to date'.
Always update Last updated to today's date when changes are made.
fingerprints.json/dep_graph.json updates are OPTIONAL — only run those steps if the files exist; gracefully skip otherwise (backward compat).
Atomic writes for fingerprints.json/dep_graph.json: read full file → apply all changes in memory → write complete file. Never partial appends.
dep_graph reverse-edge maintenance: when modifying file F, for each old import not in new — remove F from target's imported_by; for each new import not in old — add F to target's imported_by.
patterns: Three-phase incremental update: diff detection → parallel scan → TOC apply.
Multi-source change detection: git history + content hash + fingerprint date.
Semantic change classification: PURPOSE / API / SIGNATURE / DEPENDENCY.
Atomic JSON updates with full read-modify-write.
conventions: Old fingerprint included in agent prompt for fingerprinted modified files.
Old TOC entry included in agent prompt for content-hash-modified files.
